pullRequest.title: Fix incorrectly-named test suite function
pullRequest.body:
No CHANGELOG needed for a change this small.

Closes #1102

pullRequest.title: fix: close HTTPError to prevent ResourceWarning on Python 3.14
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Explicitly close `urllib.error.HTTPError` in `PyJWKClient.fetch_data()` to prevent `ResourceWarning` on Python 3.14
- `HTTPError` is both a `URLError` subclass and a response-like object (inheriting from `tempfile._TemporaryFileWrapper` via `addinfourl`), so it must be closed to release the underlying resource
- Added test to verify `HTTPError` is properly closed when HTTP errors are encountered

Fixes #1128

## Reproduction

```python
import jwt
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings('default')

client = jwt.PyJWKClient("https://dev-example.us.auth0.com/.well-known/jwks.json")
try:
    client.get_signing_keys()
except jwt.PyJWKClientConnectionError:
    pass

# Before fix (Python 3.14):
# ResourceWarning: Implicitly cleaning up <HTTPError 404: 'Not Found'>
#
# After fix: no warning
```

## Test plan
- [x] New test `test_http_error_is_closed_on_connection_failure` verifies `HTTPError.closed` is `True` after `fetch_data()` handles the error
- [x] All 334 existing tests continue to pass

pullRequest.title: Use PyJWK algorithm when encoding without explicit algorithm
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes a regression where encoding with a PyJWK key would ignore the key's embedded algorithm and default to HS256 instead. Now when no algorithm is explicitly specified, the encoder will use the algorithm from the PyJWK object.

## Changes
- Modified `PyJWS.encode()` to check if the key is a PyJWK instance and use its algorithm when no algorithm parameter is provided
- Modified `PyJWT.encode()` to apply the same logic for JWT encoding
- Introduced `_ALGORITHM_UNSET` sentinel value to distinguish between "no algorithm specified" (use key's algorithm) and "algorithm explicitly set to None" (use key's algorithm as fallback)
- Added regression tests in both `test_api_jws.py` and `test_api_jwt.py` to verify the fix (issue #1147)

## Implementation Details
- The sentinel value `_ALGORITHM_UNSET` replaces the previous default of `"HS256"` to allow proper detection of when an algorithm was explicitly provided vs. when it should be inferred from the key
- When `algorithm is _ALGORITHM_UNSET`, the code now checks if the key is a PyJWK and uses `key.algorithm_name`; otherwise defaults to `"HS256"`
- The existing behavior for explicitly passing `algorithm=None` is preserved as a fallback mechanism

https://claude.ai/code/session_016Ekc2jQzpuiDpBvnMAMnUB

pullRequest.title: Add typing_extensions dependency for Python < 3.11
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Add `typing_extensions` as a conditional dependency for Python versions below 3.11 to support type hints that are only available in newer Python versions.

## Changes
- Added `typing_extensions >= 4.0` as a dependency for Python versions < 3.11
- This ensures compatibility with type annotation features that were introduced in Python 3.11 while maintaining support for earlier Python versions (3.9+)

## Details
The `typing_extensions` package provides backports of typing features from newer Python versions, allowing the codebase to use modern type hints across all supported Python versions (3.9+).

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ZvbKEyVkcUx8xHrqt8v2F
